{147}------------------------------------------------

### <span id="page-147-0"></span>Risk management

### Board of Directors

Approval of the Risk Management and Control Policy, which establishes the basic principles, key risk factors and the general framework for their management.

| Audit and Compliance Committee                                                                                                                                                          | Senior Management                                                                                                       |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Supervision                                                                                                                                                                             | Awareness and education                                                                                                 |
| of the risk control and management function, verifying its operation<br>based on the policy approved by the Board.                                                                      | of the importance of the Integrated Risk Management<br>System and its value for all the Company's stakeholders.         |
| Assessment                                                                                                                                                                              | Definition and validation                                                                                               |
| of the effectiveness of financial and non-financial risk internal<br>control and management systems, as well as the measures<br>planned to mitigate the impact of the risks identified. | of roles, attributions, and responsibilities within the<br>framework of the Integrated Risk Management System.          |
| Identification and re-assessment                                                                                                                                                        | Validation of action plans                                                                                              |
| at least annually, of the most significant financial and non-financial<br>risks and their tolerance levels.                                                                             | and work plans derived from the risk management<br>process itself, and activity monitoring.                             |
| Risk Map                                                                                                                                                                                | Setting the risk level                                                                                                  |
| identifying the main risks by category, and an evaluation of these in<br>terms of their potential impact, probability and the Group's<br>preparedness for tackling them.                | that the Company considers acceptable, based on the<br>objectives and interests of the Company and its<br>stakeholders. |

### Three lines of defence

### 1. Business units *(continuous reporting)*

Responsible for managing and reporting the risks to which the Group is exposed in their respective areas, including climate risk.

### 2. Risk management / Compliance function *(quarterly reporting)*

Responsible for coordinating and updating the Integrated Risk Management System to maintain the highest level of quality, coordinate the management of non-compliance risks (detailed in 8.1.2. Global Compliance Model and Criminal Risk Prevention Model).

### 3. Internal Audit *(quarterly reporting)*

Independently and objectively supervising the Integrated Risk Management System.

### Risk identification, assessment and prioritisation

There are uniform, standardised and systematic processes for identifying, evaluating, and prioritizing risks, based on the concepts of risk appetite, risk tolerance and risk target. The risk factors to which the Group is subject are classified into six categories, which are subdivided into lower hierarchical classifications according to their causality: financial, geopolitical, technological, environmental, social and governance risks.

The risk identification process aims to pinpoint, recognise and describe the risks that may prevent the Company from achieving its objectives. The identification is based on the best available information, taking as a fundamental basis the knowledge and expertise of the areas directly responsible for risk management, supplemented, if necessary, with relevant external sources. Emerging risks are also considered, i.e., those new risks that are in the process of transformation or are a novel combination of risks, whose impact, likelihood of occurrence and cost are not yet well understood. In any case, the geographical scope or dimension of the risks is considered and evaluated, especially those particular to certain geographies and those related to climate.

{148}------------------------------------------------

Risk criteria must be set by Senior Management, based on the Group's objectives and interests, as well as those of our various stakeholders, and are updated periodically. Risks are assessed in terms of residual risk, i.e., the risk remaining after taking mitigation actions.

The assessment considers three magnitudes for each risk: impact, likelihood of occurrence and level of preparedness. The Risk Management department periodically (at least annually) asks the different risk management units to assess and review the different risks and the mitigation and adaptation measures implemented and planned through a

Image /page/148/Picture/4 description: A close-up shot depicts a pair of hands gently resting on a smooth, pink surface. The hands are positioned one above the other, with the fingers slightly curved and relaxed. A cylindrical object, possibly a bar or rod, is situated between the hands and the pink surface. The background features a series of vertical lines or threads, suggesting a textile or weaving context. The lighting is soft and diffused, emphasizing the texture of the hands and the fabric.

system of interviews and questionnaires. In general, risks are assessed on a one-year time horizon, although, for some risk categories, particularly environmental risks such as climate risk, short, medium and long-term time horizons are considered. A risk register is kept, represented in a risk map, evaluated according to their total impact (strong, high, moderate and minor risks). The map is periodically reported to the Board of Directors and contains the critical risks, meaning those which, if they 'were to materialise' could jeopardize the achievement of our strategic objectives.

### Enterprise risk management and control framework

### Impact

Effect that a risk would have if it were to materialise. Risk managers consider the worst possible impact scenario for the materialisation of the risk and assess the impact on each strategic objective based on their own calculations, except for the 'Corporate image and reputation' variable, for which they use a standard questionnaire. To obtain the total risk impact assessment, the result of the variable with the highest impact is considered and increased according to the other affected variables on a weighted basis.

### Likelihood

Risk managers consider an average scenario to assess the probability of occurrence. The estimated probability of the risk materialising is assessed taking into account the track record of the past five years as well as the one-year expectations. The different possible scenarios are documented in terms of impact and likelihood of occurrence.

### Preparation

The level of preparedness is assessed using a questionnaire on aspects related to response capacity, existing mechanisms and controls, scenario analysis and contingency plans.

*These assessments are transferred to the tolerance scales defined by Senior Management for each of the variables to obtain the total impact level. Currently, different thresholds are evaluated for the following financial variables: change in sales, change in gross margin, change in net profit and change in cash flow generated from operating activities.*

{149}------------------------------------------------

<span id="page-149-0"></span>Image /page/149/Figure/2 description: The image is a risk assessment matrix. The y-axis is labeled "- Probability of occurrence +" and the x-axis is labeled "- Impact +". The matrix is divided into four quadrants. The top left quadrant is labeled "Moderate", the top right quadrant is labeled "Strong", the bottom left quadrant is labeled "Minor", and the bottom right quadrant is labeled "High".

For the assessment of climate change related risks, we also use methods aimed at the financial assessment of physical (acute and chronic) and transitional risks in the short, medium and long term using a scenario methodology.

i More information in section *[6.1.5. Risks and opportunities arising from](#page-202-0)  [climate change](#page-202-0)* of this Report.

The Group is currently in a process of progressive transition in the measurement of critical non-climate related risks, from an impact, likelihood of occurrence and level of preparedness methodology to a scenario methodology.

Each scenario is analysed considering increasing levels of severity to simulate its likelihood of occurrence, its temporal evolution, its recovery curve and its aggregate and disaggregated impact. The purpose is to calculate 'Earning Value at Risk' over a five-year time horizon discounted to obtain its present value. This enables the Group to assess risks in intrinsic and residual terms, once risk mitigation and transfer measures have been taken into account. This method corresponds to the process of evolution and development of the IRMS.

### 5.1.3.2. Risk map

The risk map represents the inventory of critical risks for the Group. There are also maps for specific risk categories that offer greater granularity.

mitigation measures and the trend with respect to the previous year.

|                     | The euro is the Group's functional currency. Our<br>international transactions require the use of<br>numerous currencies, which gives rise to foreign<br>currency exchange risk. Currency exposure<br>emerges in terms of net investment, translation<br>and transaction risks. We have investments<br>overseas whose assets are exposed to the foreign<br>currency exchange risk. And given that we<br>consolidate the annual accounts of our<br>companies in euros, we are exposed to foreign<br>exchange translation risk resulting from all our<br>entities located outside the Eurozone.<br>We are also exposed to the risk arising from the<br>volatility in currencies other than the euro of<br>payment and collection flows in relation to the<br>acquisition and provision of goods and services in<br>both Group and non-Group transactions. | / Financial Risk Management Policy.<br>ⓘ More information in note 26. Financial instruments and risk<br>management policy of the Consolidated Annual Accounts.                                                                                                                                                                                           | →          |
| Market crisis       | Supply and distribution shipping is critical to our<br>business. There is a risk of stoppage or delay in<br>the movement and customs clearance of goods<br>as a result of changes in the political/social<br>situation and stability in markets where goods are<br>produced and sold, regulatory changes, trade<br>frictions—whether tariff or non-tariff related, and<br>saturation of logistics infrastructures, among<br>others.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | / In-house teams specialised in market customs<br>regulations, which are in permanent contact with customs<br>agencies.<br>/ Diversification of shipping points and establishment of<br>alternative routes.<br>/ Continuous tracking of the product until it arrives at the<br>store.<br>/ Monitoring of relevant regulations and regulatory<br>changes. | →          |
| Economic<br>outlook | Our activity is subject to the risk of a potential<br>downturn in sales as a result of economic<br>contraction or other macroeconomic headwind<br>generated by external factors.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | / Flexible business model based on multi-brand omni-<br>channelling.<br>/ Territorial diversification through internationalisation<br>strategy.<br>ⓘ More information in sections 3. Inditex at a glance and 5.2.<br>Strategy of this Report.                                                                                                            | →          |

{154}------------------------------------------------

|-----------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------|
| Economic<br>variables | The Group is exposed to the risk of inflation<br>affecting costs linked to the acquisition of the<br>goods and services necessary to conduct our<br>business. It is worth noting the impact of the<br>increase in the price of the multiple raw materials,<br>(textile and non-textile) consumed directly and<br>indirectly in our operations, and in the procurement<br>of goods, primarily of our products and services,<br>particularly in relation to the transportation of<br>supplies and distribution. | / Flexibility of the manufacturing and procurement model,<br>allowing production to be adapted to market demand and<br>to possible changes in the supply market environment.                                        |                        |
|                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | / Permanent contact between stores and online teams with<br>our team of designers, through the Product Management<br>department, helping them to learn about customer<br>preferences.                               |                        |

### GEOPOLITICAL RISKS

Geopolitical risks arise from a deterioration in the political situation, a society's crime levels, changes in the ideology, leadership and regulation of its authorities, politically motivated conflicts at home or between nation states that threaten operations or expected prospects.

| Main risks                                              | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Risk trend |
|---------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Business<br>environment<br>and political<br>instability | Potential instability in the territories where our supply<br>chain is located, as well as where products are<br>marketed, represents a significant risk. Socio-political<br>instability arising from social uprisings or other causes<br>of political violence, as well as their potential spread to<br>other countries, may affect our ability to operate in<br>affected territories, with the consequent impact on<br>value chains, sales and expansion, or damage to our<br>facilities.<br>These circumstances may result in frictions that hinder<br>the normal movement of goods due to political<br>instability, infrastructure saturation, or constraints,<br>especially on key routes, which generate bottlenecks<br>due to mismatches between supply and demand that<br>limit access to transport and/or erode business<br>margins. | / Value chain with multiple geographic origins,<br>providing the necessary flexibility and adaptation to<br>demand, guaranteeing alternatives to the eventual<br>need to switch between different manufacturing<br>markets in the event of continued severe disruptions.<br>/ Points of sale diversification.<br>/ Country risk monitoring and proximity to local<br>markets.<br>/ Continuous analysis of the evolution of the conflict<br>and its complex implications, implementing plans to<br>mitigate its impact, especially in relation to its<br>workforce in the affected markets.<br>ⓘ More information in sections 3. Inditex at a glance, 5.2.<br>Strategy and 8.3. Supplier relations of this Report. | →          |

|------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Government<br>and business<br>policies, and<br>regulatory<br>framework | As a result of our extensive direct and indirect<br>geographic presence, we are exposed to multiple<br>and heterogeneous legislation in the countries where we operate. Regulatory changes, which are increasingly frequent and more intense, especially in our industry, as well as the possibility that local authorities might adopt different or even divergent interpretations in various jurisdiction, expose the Group to potential negative effects of a financial, compliance and/or reputational nature. | / Systematic monitoring of the impacts and risks of<br>emerging regulations that affect the business model<br>and the proposal of operational solutions.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | →          |
|                                                                        | This includes risks relating to labour, commercial and<br>consumer law, industrial and intellectual property law,<br>and personal data protection and privacy regulations,<br>with particular relevance on tax and customs<br>regulations, as well as risks relating to other<br>legislations.                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | / Business model based on a firm commitment to good<br>governance, transparency and respect, aimed at<br>promoting social and environmental sustainability,<br>and spreading a corporate ethical culture in the performance of all our activities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | →          |
|                                                                        | Expectations of significant regulatory or policy<br>changes that may create uncertainty for the normal development of the business model and require a financial and operational planning to ensure proper adaptation.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | / Continuous Compliance training.<br><br>/ Existence of an independent body that supervises compliance with the Internal Regulations of conduct (Ethics Committee) and a mechanism to report irregularities and non-compliances (Ethics Line).<br><br>/ Implementation of the model for the organisation, prevention, detection, control and management of legal and reputational risks, arising from potential non-compliance.<br><br>/ Policy on Donations and Sponsorships, Policy on Gifts and Invitations, and Policy on Dealings with Public Officials.<br><br>/ Tax Policy.<br><br>/ Anti-Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing Policy.<br><br>/ Continuous assessment of the corporate governance system in order to verify its degree of compliance with and adaptation to new legislation recommendations | →          |

### GOVERNANCE RISK

Governance risk includes non-compliance by the Company and, in particular, by its Board of Directors and Senior Management, with the law in a formal and/or material sense, with good governance recommendations, best practices, as well as the commitments that we voluntarily undertake. It also includes risks arising from the tactical and strategic decisions of the Group's management that may result in the non-achievement of business, functional area or Group objectives, as well as risks of corruption or damage to the Company's reputation.

| Main risks                       | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Risk trend |
|----------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Business model<br>implementation | Risks related to potential<br>failure in the commercial offer<br>by not anticipating trends, not<br>being able to react and<br>respond to changes in market<br>trends, not providing sufficient<br>supply to stores or not being<br>able to continue to stand out<br>from competitors. | / Agility and flexibility of the business model.<br>/ Qualified teams focused on identifying the product demanded by the<br>market and ensuring adequate supply management.<br>/ Availability of reliable data, with the necessary frequency and level of<br>detail, providing information on the adequacy of supply to market demand.<br>/ Linking up with customers through social media and existence of different<br>points of contact to capture tastes and expectations (stores, online sales).<br>/ Sourcing in proximity allowing a quick response to customer demand.<br>ⓘ More information in section 5.2. Strategy of this Report.                     | →          |
|                                  | Risk of concentration of<br>logistics operations<br>(procurement, storage and<br>distribution) in a limited<br>number of distribution<br>centres, both own or operated<br>by third parties, located<br>across Spain.                                                                   | / Logistics Hub in the Netherlands.<br>/ Use of smaller logistics centres located in other countries and operated<br>with third parties which conduct variable scale distribution operations.<br>/ Implementation of our WMS (Warehouse Management System) in all<br>external logistics operators to ensure full operability and compatibility with<br>our systems.<br>/ Our RFID and SINT (Integrated Stock Management System) programmes<br>have been implemented in all retail formats.<br>/ Logistics Expansion Plan, which assesses the need and contemplates<br>new investments if required.<br>ⓘ More information in section 5.2. Strategy of this Report. | ↑          |

{157}------------------------------------------------

| Main risks     | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Risk trend |
|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Non-Compliance | The Group is exposed to the<br>risk of non-compliance with<br>its Policy on Human Rights<br>and other regulations we have<br>established in this matter. In<br>particular, non-compliance<br>with our Code of Conduct for<br>Manufacturers and Suppliers,<br>defined as the minimum<br>standards of ethical and<br>responsible behaviour to be<br>observed by the<br>manufacturers and suppliers<br>of the products we market.<br>Furthermore, the demand for<br>ESG (environmental, social<br>and governance) behaviour in<br>the fashion industry is<br>increasing and is based on<br>traceability and transparency. | / Mandatory compliance with the Code of Conduct for Manufacturers and<br>Suppliers by all those who wish to become part of the Group's supply<br>chain.<br>/ Code of Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers compliance<br>programme through different types of audits of the facilities required for<br>production.<br>/ Ethics Line and Ethics Committee (also responsible for the application and<br>interpretation of the Code of Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers).<br>/ Traceability strategy based on a management system whereby each<br>supplier is required to know and share its supply chain data and report the<br>facilities involved in the productions, and an verification process that<br>confirms that production takes place in declared and authorised factories.<br>/ The Group's commitment to transparency. We share information with our<br>stakeholders concerning our sustainability strategy, the programmes on<br>which is it articulated, our objectives and their progress, among other<br>topics. One of our main purposes of being transparent is the annual<br>reporting that we conduct through this Statement on Non-Financial<br>Information, or the information that we regularly disclose on our website.<br>ⓘ More information in sections 5.1.2. Human Rights due diligence, 8.1. Corporate<br>ethical culture and solid compliance architecture and 8.3. Supplier relations of this<br>Report and section F.1.2. of the Annual Corporate Governance Report. | ↑          |

| Non-compliance           | Our Group faces the risk of<br>non-compliance with the law<br>in a formal or material sense,<br>with good governance<br>recommendations, best<br>practices or voluntary<br>commitments.<br><br>In this regard, there is a risk of<br>non-compliance with<br>voluntary commitments that<br>have been publicly<br>undertaken, especially in the<br>area of sustainability, which<br>exceed regulatory<br>requirements.<br><br>This includes risks related to<br>tax, customs, anti-corruption<br>and bribery, labour law,<br>commerce and consumption-<br>related regulations, industrial<br>and intellectual property<br>regulations and risks relating<br>to other types of legislation, in<br>particular criminal regulatory<br>risks, as well as other<br>regulatory compliance risks. | / Global Compliance Model.<br><br>/ Criminal Risk Prevention Model, comprising the Criminal Risk Prevention<br>Policy, the Criminal Risk Prevention Procedure and the Criminal Risk and<br>Control Matrix.<br><br>/ The main policies approved within the framework of the Criminal Risk<br>Prevention Model are as follows: Policy on Donations and Sponsorships,<br>Policy on Gifts and Invitations, Policy on Dealings with Public Officials,<br>Conflicts of Interest Policy, Anti-Money Laundering and Terrorist Financing<br>Policy, Due Diligence Policy and the Procedure for Limiting Trade<br>Relations with Suppliers in Restricted or Unauthorised Markets.<br><br>/ Ethics Line and Ethics Committee.<br><br>Image: More information in section 8.1.2 Global Compliance Model and Criminal Risk<br>Prevention Model of this Report.<br><br>/ Data protection and privacy compliance model, considering the<br>Compliance Policy Regarding Data Protection and Privacy as the basic<br>rule.<br><br>Image: More information in section 8.2.2. Personal Data Protection and Privacy of this<br>Report.<br><br>/ Annual assessment of the Corporate Governance System in order to<br>verify its degree of compliance and adaptation to new legislation,<br>recommendations, standards and best practices in this respect, and to<br>systematically reinforce good corporate governance practices.<br><br>/ Regular supervision by the Audit and Compliance Committee of the<br>existence of possible conflicts of interest or transactions with/among<br>related parties, contrary to the interests of the Company and/or its<br>stakeholders.<br><br>/ Quarterly monitoring by the Sustainability Committee, of the progress on<br>the commitments undertaken. | Image: Right Arrow |

### <span id="page-159-0"></span>ENVIRONMENTAL RISKS

Risks associated with natural disasters, climate change and the interactions resulting from human exploitation of the environment.

| Main risks                                                           | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Risk trend |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Climate change                                                       | Our performance is exposed to<br>the potential impacts of climate<br>change in its different<br>manifestations of physical risk,<br>whether chronic or acute, as<br>well as to the risks resulting<br>from the transition to a low-<br>carbon economy. For physical<br>risks, seven climate<br>phenomena are taken into<br>consideration: heatwave,<br>freeze, river flooding, coastal<br>flooding, 'temperate' and<br>tropical storms and water<br>stress in the river basins where<br>our own or third-party facilities<br>are located.                                                                                                                                      | / Decarbonisation is one of the main pillars of our Sustainability<br>Roadmap, in line with the goals of the Paris Agreement. We have set<br>very ambitious decarbonisation targets for our business and for our<br>industry as a whole.<br>/ Section 6.1.5. Risks and opportunities arising from climate change<br>focuses on this risk under the Task Force on Climate-related Financial<br>Disclosures (TCFD), to which the Group adhered in June 2020.<br>♻ More information in sections 6.1.3.Emissions of GHG of scopes 1, 2 and 3 and<br>6.1.5. Risks and opportunities arising from climate change of this Report.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       | →          |
| Environmental<br>degradation and<br>Scarcity of natural<br>resources | There is a risk of producing<br>adverse effects on the<br>environment through the<br>release of undesirable or<br>hazardous substances<br>(whether biological or<br>chemical) throughout our value<br>chain. There is also a risk that<br>our activities may result in<br>negative externalities such as<br>the loss of biodiversity,<br>deforestation, soil degradation,<br>or scarcity of raw materials,<br>especially those that meet our<br>sustainability requirements,<br>among others. There is a need<br>to ensure sufficient and reliable<br>supply of lower-impact raw<br>materials, known as preferred<br>raw materials, to meet our<br>sustainability commitments. | / The Sustainability Policy sets out, among others, the environmental<br>commitments, which are applied transversally across all its business<br>areas and throughout the supply chain.<br>/ Environmental strategies: Biodiversity Strategy, Global Water<br>Management Strategy and Global Energy Strategy.<br>/ Forest Product Policy.<br>/ Commitment to clean energy, lower-impact raw materials and the<br>implementation of circularity services such as Zara Pre-Owned in key<br>markets.<br>/ Implementation of waste management systems with strict operating<br>requirements to allow us converting waste generated at our facilities<br>into resources available for reuse or recycling.<br>/ Forwarding for reuse and/or recycling of the packaging materials<br>arriving at our facilities.<br>/ Research and development of new materials and production<br>processes with a lower impact through the corporate platform<br>Sustainability Innovation Hub.<br>/ Collaboration projects with third parties in the field of circularity and<br>innovation (MIT Spain, Ellen MacArthur Foundation, Global Fashion<br>Agenda, Euratex, etcetera).<br>/ Application of the Green to Wear standard in the supply chain.<br>♻ More information in section 6. Environment of this Report. | →          |

| Extreme weather<br>events | As a result of natural disasters<br>such as floods, fires,<br>earthquakes, etc., key business<br>operations and shipping<br>processes could be halted.<br>These events could potentially<br>affect our critical infrastructure. | Management of these risks, including mitigation measures and<br>resilience plans, is discussed in the section on industrial accident risks.<br>Section 6.1.5. <i>Risks and opportunities arising from climate change</i><br>includes a sub-section concerning the physical climate impact on the<br>Group's value chain. | Image: Right arrow |

### TECHNOLOGY RISKS

This includes targeted cyberattacks, collapse of critical infrastructure, industrial accidents with direct or indirect impacts, as well as the inability to adapt to technological advances.

| Main risks                 | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | Risk trend |
|----------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Critical<br>infrastructure | We are exposed to the risk<br>arising from various<br>contingencies (incidents,<br>sabotage or accidents) that lead<br>to a halt or operational<br>inefficiency of IT services or<br>processes needed to perform<br>the business activity. | / Ensuring the availability of the systems by deploying technical contingency<br>plans which, together with associated technical recovery procedures and<br>their relevant recovery testing, would reduce the consequences of an<br>incident, breakdown or shutdown. | →          |
|                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | / Tier IV certified main data centre, which guarantees maximum reliability and<br>high availability.                                                                                                                                                                 |            |
|                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | / Availability of certified data centres guaranteeing high availability as well as<br>synchronous data storage in redundant locations or duplication of<br>equipment and lines.                                                                                      |            |
|                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | / Information Security Procedure: Incident Response Plan, which includes the<br>management of incidents involving personal data from the point of view of<br>compliance with personal data protection and privacy regulations.                                       |            |
|                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | ⓘ More information in section 8.2.Information security and privacy of this Report.                                                                                                                                                                                   |            |

{161}------------------------------------------------

| Main risks    | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Risk trend |
|---------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------|
| Cybersecurity | This refers to exposures that<br>could compromise the<br>continuity of operations and/or<br>the confidentiality, integrity and/<br>or availability of our information,<br>regardless of whether it is<br>located in our own systems or<br>those of third parties; and third<br>parties' information located in<br>our systems.<br>We are aware that technological<br>risks evolve exponentially,<br>unpredictably and, in some<br>cases, in a very sophisticated<br>way. Thus, although information<br>security is a priority, there is the<br>possibility of an undetectable<br>attack which might affect<br>operations or information<br>managed by us. | / Existence of a Chief Information Security Officer (or 'CISO'), reporting<br>directly to the Chief Executive Officer.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Creation of a Cybersecurity Advisory Committee, composed of independent<br>experts, with the aim of reinforcing the Group's cybersecurity decision-<br>making.                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Availability of a reference framework (Information Security Policy) aimed at<br>ensuring the confidentiality, integrity and availability of information.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Permanent control of the Information Security Management System to<br>ensure confidentiality and integrity of information and uninterrupted<br>development of the operations through the Information Security<br>Department and with the support of the Information Security Committee.                                                                                               |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Continuous review mechanisms, by the Information Security department,<br>evaluated by internal and external audits, for the prevention, detection and<br>response to cyberattacks.                                                                                                                                                                                                    |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Updating the inventory and technology and information security risk map to<br>establish the necessary mitigation measures and ensure continuous<br>improvement by the responsible areas.                                                                                                                                                                                              | →          |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Development of a strategic plan with international experts in order to<br>maintain and improve the maturity of the Information Security programme.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Insurance policies to cover loss of profit, expenses arising from the attack<br>and the Company's civil liability for damage caused to third parties.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Compliance with Payment Card Industry Data Security Standard (PCI-DSS)<br>and ISO/IEC 27001 certification for Information Security.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Certifications required within international regulatory frameworks, such as<br>the K-ISMS(1) in South Korea and the MLPS(2) in China.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Awareness and skill-building through employee cybersecurity training.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | / Procedure on Information Security Incident Response, which includes the<br>management of incidents involving personal data from the point of view of<br>compliance with data protection and privacy.                                                                                                                                                                                  |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | ⓘ More information in section 8.2. Information security and privacy of this<br>Report.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |            |
|               | (1) K-ISMS is South Korea's information security management system. This standard is managed by the Korean Internet and<br>Security Agency (KISA). It was prepared to evaluate whether enterprises and organisations operate and manage their information<br>security system consistently and securely such that they protect key information assets from various threats.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |            |
|               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | (2) MLPS is the multi-layer protection scheme. It is a regulatory classification scheme intended to protect the security of information<br>systems located in China. China's Cybersecurity Law requires the network and system components to be protected against<br>disruptions, damage, unauthorised access using a graded scale to prevent data leakage, manipulation and espionage. |            |

| Main risks               | Description and impact                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Risk trend              |
|--------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| Industrial<br>accident   | We face the risk of business<br>interruption associated with the<br>possibility of extraordinary<br>events beyond our control (fires,<br>transport or key supplier strikes,<br>interruptions in energy and fuel<br>supplies, etc.), which could<br>significantly affect the normal<br>functioning of our operations.<br>The main risks of this type are<br>concentrated at logistics centres<br>and third party operators<br>transporting goods.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   | / Actions to reduce exposure to this type of risks, maintaining high levels of<br>prevention and protection at all the distribution centres. The digital<br>transformation process, which is<br>paramount to ensure our<br>competitiveness, is gaining pace<br>due to the emergence and<br>implementation of new<br>technologies such as Artificial<br>Intelligence and its application to<br>the automation and optimisation<br>of existing processes, or its<br>transformative application in<br>other fields. | / Digital transformation and the promotion of digitalisation as a key<br>transformation tool are evident throughout all our Group's operational and<br>business processes.
