(1) The risk trend reflected here corresponds with the short term.

### Transition risks

Transition risks are financial and reputation risks associated with the transition to a low-carbon economy. These risks take into account the nature, speed and trend of changes in policies, legal frameworks, technologies, reputation and market.

Transition risks vary significantly depending on the level of ambition of each pathway analysed and affect all areas of our business.

To calculate their financial impact, we have examined five dimensions of transition risks in the short, medium and long term:

- / Regulatory
- / Legal liability
- / Technology
- / Market / Reputation

{211}------------------------------------------------

| Transition dimension: Regulatory                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Impact on business model                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Model assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Establishing an explicit carbon<br>price is a key mechanism to<br>incentivise the transition to a low-<br>carbon economy. These policies<br>are currently determined and<br>implemented both at national and<br>regional level. The aim is to<br>gradually obtain some degree of<br>international coordination. As a<br>result, the carbon prices used in<br>our models vary from country to<br>country with the aim of covering<br>all global emissions by 2025. | In this scenario, the Group would<br>pay a price for the emissions<br>generated throughout its value<br>chain. The Group's carbon footprint<br>in each of the countries in which it<br>operates is considered and the<br>carbon price in each jurisdiction is<br>applied. This includes scope 1<br>(direct emissions from sources<br>owned by the Company), scope 2<br>(indirect emissions from the<br>electricity purchased) and scope 3<br>(other indirect emissions related to<br>the Group's value chain).<br><br>Impact on cost: the Group's<br>financial impact stems from the<br>increase in production and<br>distribution costs, and the cost of<br>raw material procurements, in terms<br>of the increase per unit of product. | / The scenario applies greenhouse<br>gas (GHG) emissions in accordance<br>with production, defined as fossil<br>fuel consumption in the country for<br>the industrial production of goods<br>and services, as well as energy<br>generation.                              | / Group Sustainability<br>Roadmap that reflects<br>Inditex's firm commitment to<br>progressing towards a low-<br>carbon economy model. The<br>goal of net zero greenhouse<br>gas emissions by 2040,<br>science-based<br>decarbonisation targets<br>(SBTs) by 2030, and the<br>commitment to using 100%<br>renewable electricity at our<br>own facilities, achieved in<br>2022. |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | / All emissions are subject to a<br>carbon price. The price for different<br>economic sectors is given<br>separately.                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | / The increased costs associated with<br>carbon price mechanisms are<br>transmitted through the Group's<br>routine overheads, i.e. its general<br>expenses, distribution expenses and<br>raw materials costs.                                                            | ⓘ More information in section<br>8.3.3. Monitoring, assessment<br>and continuous improvement<br>of this Report.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | / There are no financial or fiscal<br>incentives or benefits derived from<br>carbon price revenue. Public<br>administrations allocate most of the<br>revenues to environmental<br>expenditures, the economic benefits<br>of which are not incorporated in this<br>model. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | / Suppliers are assumed to pass on<br>100% of their cost increases to us.<br>The Group passes on a portion of<br>these to its end customers.                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

{212}------------------------------------------------

| Transition dimension:<br>Legal liability                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  | Impact on business model                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | Model assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| This scenario considers<br>developments in climate-related<br>litigation, a consequence of<br>scientific advances that allow<br>climate change to be linked to<br>specific events, paving the way for<br>potential attributions of liability.<br>The scenario assumes that major<br>lawsuits are filed against the<br>Group, claiming damages based<br>on its relative contribution to<br>global greenhouse gas emissions.<br>As lawsuits start to proliferate in<br>different jurisdictions, initially in the<br>most emissions-intensive sectors,<br>the textile industry becomes a<br>potential target for litigation. | Damages are estimated based on<br>the scale of our operations, and<br>translate into claims.<br>On average, cases are assumed to<br>take several years to be resolved.<br>Plaintiffs aim to pressure the Group<br>beyond the potential legal ruling,<br>exerting increased media and<br>reputational pressure.<br><b>Cost impact:</b> the intensity of<br>lawsuits related to GHG emissions<br>and climate change that the Group<br>may experience will vary depending<br>on the different emissions<br>pathways. Their probability of<br>occurrence and potential impact will<br>also vary depending on the<br>estimated impact (settlement, legal<br>costs, severance pay, etc.). | / The model uses a decision tree to<br>evaluate a range of potential<br>outcomes, each with a probability of<br>occurrence and leading to different<br>impacts. The model harnesses<br>historical data from other sectors<br>that serve as a 'benchmark', and<br>experts' opinions, all based on the<br>company's specific characteristics.<br>The model's output is the estimated<br>financial impact given the<br>conditional probability of each<br>outcome. | / Inditex's Sustainability Policy<br>establishes that all the<br>Group's activities will be<br>conducted in the most<br>environmentally-friendly way<br>possible, fostering the<br>conservation of biodiversity<br>and the sustainable<br>management of natural<br>resources.<br><br><span style="font-size: 8pt;">ⓘ More information in section<br/>5.2.2. Sustainability strategy of<br/>this Report.</span><br><br>/ Solid Compliance System in<br>place and a robust<br>corporate governance<br>system that ensures<br>compliance with<br>regulations, guidelines and<br>best practices in this<br>connection.<br><br><span style="font-size: 8pt;">ⓘ More information in sections<br/>8.1. Corporate ethical culture<br/>and solid Compliance<br/>architecture of this Report and<br/>F.1.2. of the Annual Corporate</span> |

Governance Report.

{213}------------------------------------------------

| Transition dimension:<br>Technology                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Impact on business model                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | Model assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| This scenario analyses how the<br>Group's competitiveness might be<br>affected by the development and<br>use of new technologies that are<br>less GHG-intensive, considering<br>their operating costs and the<br>demand for our products. The<br>Group must decide how and<br>when to invest to reduce<br>emissions in its value chain so as<br>to attain an optimal combination<br>of profitability and early adoption<br>to design its R&D strategies. | In a fast-moving technology market,<br>the Group must invest to ensure its<br>operations and infrastructure do not<br>lag behind. At the same time, it must<br>manage the potential obsolescence<br>of existing assets. The value chain<br>faces the same challenge as the<br>Group. Costs relating to the renewal<br>of the value chain are ultimately<br>expected to be passed on to the<br>Group.<br>Impact on cost: stemming from the<br>depreciation and liquidation value of<br>assets, additional CapEx and<br>increased raw materials costs. | / This model considers the costs to<br>the Company of investing in low<br>emission technologies and boosting<br>the efficiency of operating assets, as<br>well as distribution costs. Asset<br>improvements include new<br>transportation assets (trucks, etc.),<br>as well as the factory upgrades to<br>improve energy efficiency. For each<br>technology, the model assumes that<br>a portion of the total global assets<br>are updated according to the<br>different emissions pathways at a<br>specific cost.<br>/ The costs of technological<br>improvements are compared with<br>current average unit costs. The<br>model takes into account basic<br>balance sheet data in connection<br>with the Group's buildings, facilities<br>and equipment. In addition, key<br>supply chain facilities are also<br>considered. The model assumes<br>that the Group carries out<br>technology upgrades at key facilities<br>and in the Group's vehicle fleet. | / Innovation is an inherent and<br>transversal value throughout<br>the Inditex business model,<br>which is why we collaborate<br>with our suppliers and other<br>organisations to find<br>innovative solutions that may<br>be applied throughout the<br>value chain and life cycle of<br>our products. Inditex's<br>Sustainability Innovation Hub<br>is clear evidence that it is<br>seeking to foster the circular<br>economy, contribute to<br>decarbonisation and<br>maximise environmentally<br>friendly development.<br>ⓘ More information in section<br>6.4.1. Initiatives to progress<br>towards a circular model of<br>this Report. |

{214}------------------------------------------------

| Transition dimension: Market                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | ⬆                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Impact on business model                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Model assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| This scenario envisages an<br>increasing market interest in<br>sustainable products and<br>services. Certain consumer<br>segments change their shopping<br>habits to enhance their<br>environmental and social impact.<br>Carbon-intensive companies and<br>sectors are coming under<br>increasing market scrutiny.<br>Potential changes in supply and<br>demand patterns jeopardise the<br>Group's market share and cost of<br>capital. | Consumer preferences are trending<br>towards alternative products and<br>services that produce lower<br>emissions. This could lead to the<br>emergence of new competitors that<br>propose innovations that transform<br>demand, resulting in a loss of<br>market share and potentially an<br>increase in the Group's cost of<br>capital.<br><br><b>Earnings and costs impact:</b> impact<br>on demand is expressed as the loss<br>of earnings and/or failure to comply<br>with growth targets. Investor<br>sentiment translates into an<br>increase in the cost of capital and in<br>the cost of financing. The different<br>emissions pathways determine the<br>scale of these impacts. | / Market adoption rates of sustainable<br>products have been parameterized<br>in the model based on a series of<br>key attributes (market potential,<br>innovation coefficient and imitation<br>coefficients).<br><br>/ The Group's product portfolio<br>remains static over the different time<br>horizons. Sales of sustainable<br>products are growing at a moderate<br>pace in the short term. It is assumed<br>that the potential scale of<br>sustainable products does not<br>encompass all consumers, although<br>the rate of adoption varies<br>according to the level of ambition of<br>the different pathways.<br><br>/ The model assumes that changes in<br>consumption patterns affect every<br>sector. The Group is able to pass-<br>though some of the losses to<br>customers.<br><br>/ It is assumed that the impact on<br>consumer demand outweighs the<br>impact on investors, as the market<br>remains focused on industries with<br>higher emissions. | / The Group's commitment to<br>customers also implies<br>anticipating their demands in<br>matters such as diversity,<br>sustainability or<br>transparency, issues in which<br>the aim is to involve them in<br>the efforts and progress<br>made. |

| Transition dimension: Reputation  →                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Scenario                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          | Impact on business model                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Model assumptions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | Main mitigations                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| This scenario considers a context<br>in which we do not advance<br>towards a low-carbon economy.<br>The widespread frustration with<br>the failure to meet emission<br>reduction targets causes a<br>negative shift in public opinion<br>towards large companies,<br>especially in those sectors that<br>have historically been the most<br>emissions-intensive. Negative<br>sentiment is fuelled and amplified<br>by media campaigns.<br>Large consumer segments<br>engage in climate activism,<br>focusing on specific companies<br>through sustained campaigns and<br>boycotts with considerable<br>repercussion. In extreme<br>circumstances, the shift in<br>consumer sentiment unleashes a<br>deterioration in investors'<br>perception, with consequences<br>for the Group's access to markets.<br>This trend runs parallel to the<br>growth of sustainable shopping,<br>as reflected in the Market<br>scenario. When sustainable<br>alternatives gain traction, those<br>retail formats that do not adapt<br>tend to experience a significant<br>decline in customer demand. In<br>contrast to the Market dimension,<br>impacts are idiosyncratic and<br>consumers avoid the Group or<br>specific brands. | The main impact for the Group is a<br>decline in demand for its products<br>and/or brands, which varies<br>according to specific trends, with<br>the resulting loss of sales and<br>market share. Investor sentiment,<br>meanwhile, weighs on the Group's<br>share price and financial situation.<br>Financing costs (capital and debt)<br>worsen. There is a risk of becoming<br>a target for the increasingly<br>numerous 'consumer activists'. The<br>scale of the impacts depends on<br>the level of ambition of the different<br>pathways.<br><br><b>Earnings and costs impact:</b> the<br>impacts on demand are<br>materialised through the loss of<br>earnings and/or failure to comply<br>with growth targets. Market<br>sentiment translates into impacts on<br>the cost of capital and financing. | / Two key factors define the model:<br>the change in our customers'<br>preferences towards more<br>sustainable products and the level<br>of activism among consumers. The<br>model considers two opposite<br>trends. On the one hand, activism,<br>which increases inversely to the<br>level of ambition: the lower the<br>ambition, the more activism there is.<br>On the other hand, consumers'<br>preference for more sustainable<br>products, which is greater in more<br>ambitious pathways, thereby<br>reducing the demand for<br>conventional products. | / Inditex's Sustainability<br>Roadmap includes ambitious<br>targets and actions aimed at<br>achieving the long-term goal<br>as a lever of transformation.<br>The Group collaborates with<br>all the actors in the value<br>chain and with stakeholders<br>to tackle global challenges<br>from a holistic standpoint. |

(1) The risk trend reflected here corresponds with the short term.

{216}------------------------------------------------

### Climate risks over the short, medium, and long term

To estimate the short-, medium- and long-term climate-related risks, we calculated each of them as per the five emissions pathways for our Company.

Medium- and long-term risks are estimated by translating the five-year cash flow estimates from the short term to the two corresponding future horizons.

In doing so, we seek to understand how our business model would behave under the climate conditions of each pathway. In this way we can understand what impact comes from climate evolution<sup>27</sup> .

### Short-term climate risk assessment (0-5 years)

|            |                                              | No policies<br>>4°C | Current<br>policies<br>3°C | Policies<br>announced<br>2.5°C | Paris<br>Agreement<br>2°C | Paris Agreement<br>ambition<br>1.5°C |
|------------|----------------------------------------------|---------------------|----------------------------|--------------------------------|---------------------------|--------------------------------------|
| Transition | Technology                                   |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Reputation                                   |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Legal liability                              |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Market                                       |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Regulatory                                   |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
| Physical   | Disruption of facilities and physical assets |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Market disruption                            |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |
|            | Drop in raw materials                        |                     |                            |                                |                           |                                      |

Over the next five years, the financial impacts of physical risk are relatively limited and slightly lower than those reported in the previous year, mainly due to a decrease in the impacts caused by our key facilities to extreme weather events. This decrease is due mainly to changes in climate models and the improvement in the estimation of exposure in our value chain key facilities.

The frequency and severity of physical risks as a result of the step-up from CMIP5 to CMIP6 has increased. Even so, the probability that acute events could cause significant losses ('catastrophic physical tail risks') remains low at present.

n Minor n Moderate n High n Acute

The most impactful weather events for the Group are heatwaves, river flooding and drought or water stress.

In the short term, around 70 % of physical risk, in its different manifestations, comes from our own operations. The remaining 30 % corresponds to third-party transactions.

<sup>27</sup> The impacts reflected by each climate risk typology, emissions trajectory and horizon are adjusted for the probability of occurrence of each of the emissions trajectories.

n Minor n Moderate n High n Acute

### Opportunities arising from climate change<sup>28</sup>

At Inditex, we want to progress towards a better impact. In this process it is essential to reduce our exposure to climate change-related risk and, at the same time, to identify the opportunities offered to us by a low-carbon economy to ensure our Company's resilience and capacity to be a part of the transformation of our industry.

<sup>28</sup> For more information, see our responses to the CDP Climate Change questionnaires, available at www.cdp.net.

{218}------------------------------------------------

### Opportunities for Inditex in a low-carbon economy

| Opportunity                            | Description of the opportunity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|----------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Integrated business<br>model           | We continually update all our formats to introduce cutting-edge technology in our integrated platform of physical and<br>online stores, creating an efficient, sustainable and integrated economic model. We create opportunities for improvement<br>to strengthen our entire ecosystem while minimising resource consumption.<br><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif;">ⓘ</span> More information in section 5.2.1. Business model and strategy of this Report.                                                                                                        |
| Continuous strategic<br>transformation | Our integrated business model gives us a consolidated overview of our customers and their needs at all times. Our<br>strategy capitalises on this advantage to evolve our model towards economic, sustainable and integrating improvements.<br>We start by aiming to maintain the level of commercial success achieved, driven by the opportunities afforded to us by<br>digitalisation processes and our sustainability commitments.<br><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif;">ⓘ</span> More information in section 5.2.1. Business model and strategy of this Report. |
| Innovation                             | The complexity of the global challenges we face and the path towards a more sustainable model require the increasing<br>introduction of innovation, science and technology in our initiatives. For example, through our platform Sustainability<br>Innovation Hub, we work to provide the industry with new materials, manufacturing processes and initiatives to improve<br>circularity, use or end of life.<br><span style="font-family: Arial, sans-serif;">ⓘ</span> More information in section 6.4.1. Initiatives to progress towards a circular model of this Report.    |

{219}------------------------------------------------

### Opportunities for Inditex in a low-carbon economy

| Opportunity                                                                                                                   | Description of the opportunity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Customer orientation                                                                                                          | We use a process of continuous interaction with our customers as the main tool for identifying the latest trends and<br>developing products that meet their demands, whether in clothing, footwear, accessories or household products. We<br>maintain high standards through a combination of design, quality and sustainability at affordable prices. This constant<br>connection has allowed us not only to adapt to new needs, but also to gradually add new services, technologies and<br>channels.                                                                                                        |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 7.4. Our customers of this Report.                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Transformation<br>of the sector                                                                                               | We have established a unique business model distinguished by its flexibility and efficiency, constant innovation, the<br>creativity of our staff and our focus on sustainability integrated in every process involved. On that basis, we resolutely and<br>collaboratively strive to promote the transformation of the sector, generating a positive impact on society, the industry and<br>our environment.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 5.2.1. Business model and strategy of this Report.                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| New business models                                                                                                           | In keeping with our commitment to using resources more efficiently, we are developing innovative solutions that allow our<br>customers to request repairs, or sell or donate Zara garments they have at home through our Zara Pre-Owned platform,<br>already available in certain markets.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 6.4. The transition to a circular economy: resources, products and waste of this Report. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Collaboration                                                                                                                 | To address the paradigm shift that is crucial to tackle the challenges linked to the fight against climate change, circularity<br>or the sustainable development of communities, it is imperative that we join forces with all the actors involved. In this<br>connection, we take an open approach in which collaboration is a key pillar for transformation. Examples of this are our<br>partnership with entities such as the United Nations Global Compact, The Fashion Pact, Ellen MacArthur Foundation or<br>Zero Discharge of Hazardous Chemicals, among others.                                        |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 5.3.1. Stakeholder engagement of this Report.                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Efficient consumption<br>of natural resources                                                                                 | In our commitment to sustainable development, at Inditex we strongly advocate circularity, an economic, management<br>and production approach aimed at balancing growth with conserving natural resources and progressing in the<br>decarbonisation of the entire value chain. For us, circularity is a differential model of production and consumption<br>encompassing all stages from a product's design to its end of life. This approach fosters the reuse and recycling of<br>articles, extending their life cycle and minimising the use of natural resources, energy consumption and waste generation. |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 6.4. The transition to a circular economy: resources, products and waste of this Report. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Energy efficiency                                                                                                             | Energy efficiency is a priority in both our designs and our day-to-day operations. In this regard we are constantly reviewing<br>our standards to reflect cutting-edge practices and implementing new programmes to advance on the path of continuous<br>and sustainable improvement in our operations. We work closely with our suppliers and other organisations to promote<br>the rational and efficient use of energy throughout the value chain.                                                                                                                                                          |
| <i>ⓘ</i> More information in section 6.1. Climate change of this Report.                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |

{220}------------------------------------------------

### Opportunities for Inditex in a low-carbon economy

| Opportunity                         | Description of the opportunity                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
|-------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Generation of<br>renewable energies | The generation and acquisition of energy from renewable sources plays a central role in our energy strategy. For this<br>purpose, we invest in generating renewable energy at our own operating facilities. We have solar thermal, solar<br>photovoltaic or wind energy, as well as infrastructure to harness geothermal energy. This diversification reduces our<br>dependence on third parties in these aspects, and also introduces innovative concepts, such as additionality in the<br>implementation of new power generation infrastructure in the grid.<br>ⓘ More information in section 6.1.4. Lower-impact consumption and efficiency and optimisation initiatives of this Report. |
| Sustainable building                | We make the investments needed in all our headquarters, platforms and stores to oversee, reduce and mitigate the<br>impact of the consumption of resources. When building our headquarters, we follow bioclimatic criteria, encouraging the<br>installation of photovoltaic panels, the collection of rainwater for non-drinking uses and the implementation of self-<br>regulating lighting systems in accordance with outside light conditions, as specific examples of our sustainable practices.<br>ⓘ More information in section 6.1. Climate change of this Report.                                                                                                                   |

### 6.1.5.3. Climate risk management

At Inditex we manage our risks through our Risk Management and Control Policy, approved in 2020. This policy establishes our Integrated Risk Management System, which helps us to manage and control the risks that impact our Company, including those linked to climate change.

Although the management of climate risks follows general risk management principles, their specific characteristics are taken into account when it comes to assessing and quantifying them. Accordingly, we approach climate risks effectively, acknowledging their importance in the current context.

i More information in sections *[5.1.3.1. Risk management framework](#page-146-0)* and *[5.1.3.2. Risk map](#page-149-0)* of this Report.

Image /page/220/Picture/8 description: A long, modern building with many windows is surrounded by a green lawn and several trees. A sidewalk runs along the side of the building, and a wind turbine is visible in the background.

{221}------------------------------------------------

### 6.1.5.4. Metrics and targets

### Targets

At the 2023 Annual General Meeting of Inditex, we presented the latest update of our sustainability commitments which includes new and stringent pledges with a view to achieving net zero emissions by 2040.

In keeping with these targets, in 2023 we also submitted to the Science-Based Target Initiative (SBTi) the latest update of our 2030 climate commitments and our 2040 net zero emissions target for their validation. We have also devised our new Climate Transition Plan, which details our decarbonisation strategy, the resources we estimate will be needed and the collaborative initiatives we will launch to advance our commitment to addressing climate change.

### Decarbonisation mechanisms

As well as setting decarbonisation targets, we promote mechanisms to advance in their achievement, such as our variable remuneration system.

This system links our teams' remuneration to the attainment of the Company's objectives, including our sustainability commitments. For example, both our CEO and senior management have specific incentives associated with emission reductions.

i More information in section *[5.1.1. Good Corporate Governance](#page-129-0)* of this Report.

i More information in section *[6.1. Climate change](#page-189-0)* of this Report
