| Environmental<br>Impacts in the<br>Supply Chain | Percentage of tier 1 supplier facilities<br>and supplier facilities beyond tier 1 in<br>compliance with wastewater<br>discharge permits and/or<br>contractual agreement                                                                                                                                                                              | CG-AA-430a.1 | In 2023, 100% of the suppliers and 98% of the factories under the<br>scope of our Green to Wear environmental standard had authorization<br>for all their hydric resources; 100% of suppliers and 99% of the factories<br>had wastewater discharge(1) permits and in 100% of suppliers and 98%<br>of factories the direct or indirect discharge complies with the legal<br>limits or the limits agreed with the External Effluent Treatment Plant.<br>On the other hand, 88% of the suppliers and 92% of the factories under<br>the scope of our Green to Wear environmental standard, meet the<br>Foundational level of ZDHC in its direct discharges. Also, in line with our<br>commitment to ZDHC, 80% of suppliers and 67% of factories comply<br>with ZDHC limits applicable to the substances included in ZDHC<br>Wastewater Guidelines v.1.1 in its direct and indirect discharges. As a<br>consequence of the environmental audits, Corrective Action Plans are<br>carried out in those cases in which it is necessary. During these plans,<br>Inditex teams support suppliers and/or factories to correct the non<br>compliances detected, in accordance with the Company's philosophy<br>of continuous improvement of the supply chain.                                     |
|                                                 | Percentage of tier 1 supplier facilities<br>and supplier facilities beyond tier 1<br>that have completed the<br>Sustainable Apparel Coalition's Higg<br>Facility Environmental Module (Higg<br>FEM) assessment or an equivalent<br>environmental data assessment                                                                                     | CG-AA-430a.2 | All of our suppliers and manufacturers are audited against our Code of<br>Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers, which includes a section on<br>environmental compliance.<br>Specifically, 95% of the suppliers and 93% of the factories active in<br>2023 which carried out any wet process, and that therefore are under<br>the scope of our Green to Wear standard, have been environmentally<br>audited(2). These facilities process a large majority of the products that<br>include any of these wet processes.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |              | More information on pages 343, 349-350, 373-375                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Topic                                           | Accounting Metric                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Code         | Disclosure                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| Labor Conditions in<br>the Supply Chain         | Percentage of tier 1 supplier facilities<br>and supplier facilities beyond tier 1<br>that have been audited to a labor<br>code of conduct, percentage of total<br>audits conducted by a third-party<br>auditor                                                                                                                                       | CG-AA-430b.1 | All our suppliers and manufacturers are audited against our Code of<br>Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers. This verification is carried<br>out initially through a pre-assessment audit –carried out to all<br>companies before they can become part of our supply chain– and<br>subsequently periodically through social audits.<br>Specifically, 57% of the suppliers and 58% of the factories active in the<br>year 2023 were audited either through a pre-assessment or social audit<br>during this same period(3).<br>To contextualise this data, it is necessary to take into account that the<br>periodicity of the social audits varies depending on the ranking<br>obtained in the previous audit. In this sense, the interval between audits<br>of suppliers or manufacturers with A or B rankings will be longer than in<br>those with a C or D ranking. In financial year 2023, 97% of suppliers<br>active were ranked A o B.<br>In 2023, 2,111 pre-assessment audits were carried out (99% carried out<br>by external auditors), and 6,892 social audits (97% carried out by<br>external auditors). Regardless of whether the audits are performed by<br>internal or external auditors, the methodology used is both Inditex's<br>own audit methodology and SLCP's. |
|                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |              | More information on pages 343, 346-348, 373-375                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
|                                                 | Priority non-conformance rate and<br>associated corrective action rate for<br>suppliers' labor code of conduct<br>audits                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | CG-AA-430b.2 | Pages 343-348, 373-375                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
|                                                 | Description of the greatest labor and<br>environmental, health, and safety<br>risks in the supply chain                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              | CG-AA-430b.3 | Pages 150-161, 286-297                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| Raw Materials<br>Sourcing                       | List of priority raw materials; for<br>each priority raw material:<br>environmental and/or social<br>factor(s) most likely to threaten<br>sourcing, discussion on business<br>risks and/or opportunities<br>associated with<br>environmental and/or social factors,<br>and management strategy for<br>addressing business risks and<br>opportunities | CG-AA-440a.3 | Pages 150-161, 206-211, 226-228, 231-233, 236-242, 296-297, 343-345                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|                                                 | Amount of priority raw materials<br>purchased, by material, and (2)<br>amount of<br>each priority raw material that is<br>                                                                                                                                                                                                                           | CG-AA-440a.4 | Pages 239                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Activity Metrics                                | Number of tier 1 suppliers and<br>suppliers beyond tier 1                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | CG-AA-000.A  | In 2023, Inditex's supply chain comprised 1,733 suppliers - equivalent to<br>tier 1 and defined as direct suppliers with which Inditex maintains a<br>commercial relationship –which, in turn, used 8,123 factories for the<br>Group's productions– equivalent to suppliers beyond tier 1.<br>Fashion item suppliers with production of over 20,000 units in the<br>summer and winter 2023 campaigns are included. Suppliers with lower<br>productions account for 0.19% of total production.<br>Factories declared by suppliers in the product traceability systems for the orders of the summer and winter 2023 campaigns are included.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |

(1) Includes suppliers and factories assigned to suppliers with more than 20,000 purchasing units in the summer and winter 2023 seasons, which are subject to an environmental audit. Calculated as those suppliers and factories whose last environmental audit conducted during the reporting year meets the water discharge criteria included in our Green to Wear standard.

(2) Includes suppliers and factories assigned to suppliers with more than 20,000 purchasing units in the summer and winter 2023 seasons that are under environmental audit scope. Calculated as the quotient of the number of suppliers/factories that have been environmentally audited (GtW or EPA) over the total number of suppliers/ factories under environmental audit scope.

{391}------------------------------------------------

(3) Including suppliers and factories allocated to suppliers with more than 20,000 purchasing units in the summer and winter 2023 seasons. Calculated as those suppliers and factories with a pre-assessment or social audit performed during the reporting period.

{392}------------------------------------------------

|                                                                                                                                       | GRI 3: MATERIAL TOPICS 2021                                                                                                                                             | |                                                                                                                                       | 3-3 Management<br>of material topics                                                                                                                                    | 105, 107-108, 183-187, 190-224<br>Inditex has a dedicated Energy Strategy and is working to<br>minimise its impact on climate change all along its value<br>chain. To that end we are streamlining our logistics<br>processes, promoting energy efficiency in our facilities and<br>making progress on our commitment to using renewable<br>sources of energy. Further information about Inditex Global<br>Energy Strategy is available at the corporate web page of<br>Inditex, Sustainability section, Reporting heading.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |

                                                                                                         |                                                                                                          |                                                   |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 3-3 Management<br>of material topics                                                                                                                                    | 105, 107-108, 183-187, 190-192, 199-203, 225-229, 234-247                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | GRI 303: WATER AND EFFLUENTS 2018                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 303-2<br>Management of<br>water discharge-<br>related impacts                                                                                                           | 201, 225-229, 346, 349-350<br>The water supplied to all workplaces, whether for<br>processing or consumption, is withdrawn from authorised<br>public supply networks, so that Inditex does not have any<br>impact on protected habitats. Moreover, all of its water<br>supply comes from areas experiencing low or no water<br>stress.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       | Principle 8                    |
|                                                                                                                                       | GRI 305: EMISSIONS 2016                                                                                                                                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 305-6 Emissions<br>of ozone-<br>depleting<br>substances (ODS)                                                                                                           | 199-202, 343<br>We have plans in place to replace air conditioning units in<br>headquarters, distribution centres and stores with more<br>efficient Class A units which enable us to avoid the emission<br>of ozone-depleting substances. In addition, though the<br>different measures implemented in our facilities, such as the<br>renovation of climate control systems for example, the<br>energy performance and efficiency is boosted. These<br>actions to foster energy efficiency, coupled with the<br>materialisation of our commitment to renewable energy, are<br>key in our commitment to reducing the GHG emissions<br>associated with our business activities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       | Principle 7<br>y 8             |
| GRI<br>Standard                                                                                                                       | Disclosure                                                                                                                                                              | Page number(s) and/or direct answer                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Omissions                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     | External<br>Assurance | Global<br>Compact<br>principle |
|                                                                                                                                       | 305-7 Nitrogen<br>oxides (NOx),<br>sulfur oxides<br>(SOx), and other<br>significant air<br>emissions                                                                    | 201                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | Not applicable.<br>We do not disclose<br>information about<br>other air emissions<br>as they are<br>considered non-<br>material due to the<br>characteristics of the<br>devices and the<br>equipment review<br>frequency stipulated<br>in prevailing<br>legislation (controls<br>are not necessary).<br>The emission of<br>particles deriving<br>from transportation<br>is generated by<br>outsourced carriers<br>so that this indicator<br>is not applicable to<br>the Group.<br>Nevertheless, Inditex<br>fosters enhanced<br>emissions<br>management and<br>control in its value<br>chain through the<br>tool that enables<br>calculation of<br>emissions in<br>accordance with the<br>GHG Protocol,<br>coupled with the<br>definition of action<br>plans.                                                                |                       | Principle 7<br>y 8             |
| GRI 306: WASTE 2020                                                                                                                   | 306-5 Waste<br>directed to<br>disposal                                                                                                                                  | 246-247                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | The Company's<br>systems are not<br>currently capable of<br>producing<br>disclosures about<br>the waste diverted<br>from disposal in its<br>proprietary stores<br>and construction<br>and refurbishment<br>works with the level<br>of detail required.<br>Inditex has a number<br>of projects underway<br>with the aim of being<br>able to comply with<br>this disclosure<br>requirement in future<br>years.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                | √<br>Pg. 418-421      | Principle 8                    |

| WATER MANAGEMENT                                                                                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                         |                                                                                                          | |                                                                                                                                       | 3-3 Management<br>of material topics                                                                                                                                    | 105, 107-108, 160, 183-187, 223-229                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                         | The Global Water Management Strategy (available at the<br>corporate web page of Inditex, Sustainability section,<br>Reporting heading) constitutes a roadmap for sustainable<br>and rational water management, working towards better<br>conservation of the environmental quality of river and<br>marine ecosystems. Inditex is also committed to achieving<br>zero discharge of unwanted substances, which contributes<br>to the sustainability of water resources.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |

                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 303-1 Interactions<br>with water as a<br>shared resource                                                                                                                | 225-229                                                         | |                                                                                                                                       | 303-3 Water<br>withdrawal |                                                                                                                                       |                                                                                                                                                                         | Further information about water discharges, available at the<br>corporate web page of Inditex, Sustainability section,<br>Reporting heading.                                                                 | |                                                                                                                                       | 303-5 Water<br>consumption                                                                                                                                              | 226<br>Inditex discloses information about water consumption at all<br>of its offices, own factories, own logistics centres and all its<br>own stores worldwide. The water supplied to all workplaces,<br>whether for processing or consumption, is withdrawn from<br>authorised public supply networks. Moreover, all of its water<br>supply comes from areas experiencing low or no water<br>stress.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | The disclosure of<br>water consumption<br>in areas<br>experiencing water<br>stress is not<br>applicable as<br>Inditex's business is<br>to distribute fashion<br>garments (clothing,<br>footwear and<br>accessories) and<br>home textile<br>products, which it<br>procures as finished<br>products from its<br>suppliers. In short,<br>water consumption<br>is not material on<br>account of its<br>business model and<br>all of its water supply<br>is sourced from<br>areas that present<br>low or no water<br>stress. As for its<br>suppliers, the<br>Company has a<br>number of policies<br>and assessment and<br>improvement<br>programmes (e.g.,<br>Code of Conduct for<br>Manufacturers and<br>Suppliers, Green to<br>Wear, etc.) designed<br>to drive<br>environmental<br>sustainability across<br>its supply chain. | √<br>Pg. 418-421      |                                |

| BIODIVERSITY AND ECOSYSTEMS                                                                                                           |                                                                                                                                                                         | |                                                                                                                                       | GRI 3: MATERIAL TOPICS 2021                                                                                                                                             |                                                   |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 3-3 Management<br>of material topics                                                                                                                                    | 105, 107-108, 183-187, 230-233<br>In the Code of Conduct, Inditex pledges to minimise the<br>environmental impact of its products in respect of their<br>entire life cycle.<br>Inditex' Biodiversity Policy (available at the corporate web<br>page of Inditex, Sustainability section, Reporting heading)<br>sets down its biodiversity protection and conservation<br>targets, which it defined following the principles established<br>in the United Nations Convention on Biological Diversity and<br>acknowledging the work of the International Union for<br>Conservation of Nature (IUCN). To guarantee application of<br>this strategy, these principles are layered into the master<br>plans of each of the key areas comprising the business<br>model.<br>The Inditex Group applies responsible production standards<br>in relation to the use of products of animal origin. Inditex has<br>a dedicated animal welfare policy, included in the<br>Sustainability Policy, and a Biodiversity Strategy, which<br>establish management criteria throughout the value chain,<br>both documents available at the corporate web page of<br>Inditex, Sustainability section, Reporting heading. |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | GRI 304: BIODIVERSITY 2016                                                                                                                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 304-1 Operational<br>sites owned,<br>leased, managed<br>in, or adjacent to,<br>protected areas<br>and areas of high<br>biodiversity value<br>outside protected<br>areas |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    | Not applicable.<br>The sites owned by<br>Inditex are neither<br>adjacent to nor<br>located within<br>protected areas or<br>areas of high<br>biodiversity value, so<br>that its activities do<br>not have a significant<br>impact in<br>biodiversity.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |                       | Principle 8                    |
|                                                                                                                                       | 304-2 Significant<br>impacts of<br>activities, products<br>and services on<br>biodiversity                                                                              | 160, 230-233 as<br>a result there are no<br>habitats protected or<br>restored as a result<br>of its business<br>activities.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |                       | Principle 8                    |
|                                                                                                                                       | 304-4 IUCN Red<br>List species and<br>national<br>conservation list<br>species with<br>habitats in areas<br>affected by<br>operations                                   | Inditex is committed to protecting and nurturing biodiversity<br>by means of responsible and sustainable management of<br>natural resources.<br>For further information, refer to the Group's Biodiversity<br>Strategy and Forest Product Policy, available at the<br>corporate web page of Inditex, Sustainability section,<br>Reporting heading.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | Not applicable.<br>Inditex's business is<br>to distribute fashion<br>garments (clothing,<br>footwear and<br>accessories) and<br>home textiles, which<br>it procures as<br>finished products<br>from its suppliers; as<br>a result the<br>organisation's<br>operations have no<br>direct impact on any<br>habitats.<br>As for its suppliers,<br>the Company has a<br>number of<br>programmes (e.g.,<br>The List by Inditex,<br>Green to Wear, etc.)<br>designed to drive<br>environmental<br>sustainability across<br>its supply chain.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |                       | Principle 8                    |

|                                                                                                                                       | GRI 401: EMPLOYMENT 2016                                                                                                                                                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       |                                |
|                                                                                                                                       | 401-1 New<br>employee hires<br>and employee<br>turnover                                                                                                                 | Voluntary turnover at the Inditex Group was 43% (43% in<br>women, 42% in men and 110% in non-binary people). By age<br>category, turnover was 66% among the under 30s, 19%<br>among those aged between 30 and 40; and 9% for those<br>over the age of 40. Turnover varied considerably by region,<br>reaching 92% in Asia, followed by the Americas with a 65%,<br>44% in Europe (excluding Spain); and 16% in Spain.<br><br>Non voluntary turnover was 50% (50% in women, 47% in<br>men and 16% in non-binary people). By age category,<br>turnover was 75% among the under 30s, 22% among those<br>aged between 30 and 40; and 13% for those over the age of<br>40.<br><br>Turnover varied considerably by region, reaching 85% in<br>Spain, followed by Europe (excluding Spain) with a 41%, 25%<br>in the Americas, and 16% in Asia.<br><br>During 2023, 2,616 people have joined Inditex (excluding the<br>Russia, Argentina and Uruguay effect), of which 32% are<br>women and 68% are men. 100% of the hires are over the<br>age of 40. Most of them took place in Spain, with 58%;<br>followed by Asia, with 39% and the Americas, with 3% of new<br>employees.                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             | √<br>Pg. 418-421      | Principle 6                    |
|                                                                                                                                       | 401-2 Benefits<br>provided to full-<br>time employees<br>that are not<br>provided to<br>temporary or part-<br>time employees                                            | The Group provides the same benefits to temporary and<br>part-time workers as it does to full-time workers. |                                                                                                                                       | 402-1 Minimum<br>notice periods<br>regarding<br>operational<br>changes                                                                                                  | The collective bargaining agreements in force do not<br>include a minimum notice period for officially<br>communicating significant operational changes at Inditex.<br>However, whenever a significant development takes place, it<br>is notified with the advance notice stipulated in prevailing<br>legislation (article 41 of the Spanish Workers' Statute). |                                                                                                                                       | 407-1 Operations<br>and suppliers in<br>which the right to<br>freedom of<br>association and<br>collective<br>bargaining may be<br>at risk                               | 145, 283-291, 348<br>Inditex's Code of Conduct specifically addresses the right to<br>freedom of association and collective bargaining. That Code<br>applies to all of the Group's operations. The Code of<br>Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers, which applies to<br>all of the Group's suppliers, also enshrines this right. The<br>programme for supervising compliance with Inditex's Code<br>of Conduct for Manufacturers and Suppliers assesses<br>compliance with workers' right to freedom of association.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |                       | Principle 3                    |

|                                                                                                                                       | AF25 Policy and<br>practices on wage<br>deductions that<br>are not mandated<br>by law                                                                                   | Inditex does not apply wage deductions that are not<br>mandated by law.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
